sales.description:
Kingdom of Macedon, Philip II AR Tetradrachm 359-336 B.C. Amphipolis mint. Posthumous issue. Struck under Kassander, circa 316-314 BC. Laureate head of Zeus to right / ΦΙΛΙΠ-ΠOΥ, nude youth, holding palm frond, on horseback to right; aplustre below, pellet-in-Π beneath raised foreleg. Le Rider pl. 46, 18; Troxell, Studies, Group 9, 323-5; SNG ANS 738-46.  Slab: 62 grams, 85 x 60cm  NGC graded AU Strike: 4/5 Surface: 4/5 Fine Style (#8221307-001).

Ancients
MACEDONIAN KINGDOM. Philip III Arrhidaeus (323-317 BC). AR tetradrachm (25mm, 16.83 gm, 7h). NGC Choice Fine 5/5 - 3/5, marks. Side, under Abdalonymus, dated Regnal Year 13 (321/320 BC). Head of Heracles right, wearing lion skin headdress, paws tied before neck / ΦIΛIΠΠOY, Zeus enthroned left, right leg drawn back, feet on stool, eagle in outstretched right hand, grounded scepter in left; ΠY above strut. Price P125A. Rare type. Frosty backlight with a flash of amber.
